content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/mattupham/.claude/skills/ticket

Work on this Linear ticket end-to-end:

https://linear.app/mattupham/issue/MATT-142/enforce-absolute-imports-via-biome

Follow these steps in order:

**Step 0 — Sync main**
Before anything else, merge the latest `main` into the current branch so the work builds on top of all previous changes:
```
git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main
```
Resolve any conflicts, then run `pnpm install` if `package.json` changed.

**Step 1 — Plan**
Use the `mcp__linear-server__get_issue` tool (or research the ticket via its URL) to read the full ticket description, acceptance criteria, and any linked context. Then produce a detailed implementation plan: which files to touch, what changes to make, and why. Share the plan and confirm with me before writing any code.

**Step 2 — Implement**
Implement exactly what the ticket asks. Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions (no `any`, path aliases, soft deletes, `PlanetScaleDatabase`, etc.). Run `pnpm format` after changes. Add or update tests where behaviour changes.

**Step 3 — Push & PR**
Run `pnpm check` to verify everything passes. Commit the work, push the branch, and open a pull request using `gh pr create`. Fill out the PR template fully — summary bullets, test plan, and link back to the Linear ticket.

**Step 4 — Review**
Run `/review` on the PR you just opened.

**Step 5 — Address feedback**
Address every piece of feedback from the review — including non-blockers and nits. Push the fixes.

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Linear
When passing string values to tools, send the content directly without escape sequences. For example, use real newlines in markdown content rather than literal backslash-n (\n) characters.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- ticket: Plan, implement, PR, review, and fix a Linear ticket end-to-end. Argument is the Linear ticket URL.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
{"id":"MATT-142","title":"Enforce absolute imports via Biome","description":"Configure Biome to require absolute imports (via the `@/*` path alias) and disallow relative paths (`../`, `./`) across `src/` and `tests/`. The path alias is already configured in `tsconfig.json` (`"@/*": ["./src/*"]`) and wired through `components.json`, so the code already has examples of both styles — this ticket is about enforcing one style consistently.

## Why

* Absolute imports don't rot when files move between directories.
* A single convention makes grep and codemods predictable.
* Matches how shadcn generates new components (they always use `@/components/ui/...`).

## Tasks

- [ ] Decide on Biome rule: investigate `style/useImportExtensions` + `style/noCommonJs` adjacent options, or (more likely) add a custom `nursery` rule / `restricted-imports`-style config. If Biome doesn't have a built-in "no relative imports" rule, document the gap and consider a lightweight lint script as a fallback.
- [ ] Update `biome.json` with the rule enabled as `error`, scoped to `src/**` and `tests/**`.
- [ ] Migrate any existing relative imports to `@/...` form (`pnpm check --write` should auto-fix if the rule supports it).
- [ ] Verify `pnpm check` fails on a freshly introduced relative import (add a throwaway file, run check, revert).
- [ ] Note the convention in `CLAUDE.md` under "Code style" so future contributors (human + AI) follow it.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm check` green on current tree
- [ ] Adding `import { foo } from "../bar"` to any src file fails `pnpm check`
- [ ] CI still green

## Out of scope

* Deep restructuring of folder layout.
* Moving `@/components/ui/*` path aliases — those already work.","priority":{"value":4,"name":"Low"},"url":"https://linear.app/mattupham/issue/MATT-142/enforce-absolute-imports-via-biome","gitBranchName":"matt/matt-142-enforce-absolute-imports-via-biome","createdAt":"2026-04-18T22:16:55.595Z","updatedAt":"2026-04-18T22:16:55.595Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Matt Upham","createdById":"cfc4a736-aeb7-443f-96ac-d369aed20beb","assignee":"Matt Upham","assigneeId":"cfc4a736-aeb7-443f-96ac-d369aed20beb","project":"H2","projectId":"126ad417-9b35-4a01-9a5e-70cb1ceda987","team":"Matt Upham LLC","teamId":"cd0054df-5cce-421f-bcdf-cd60a59db9c9","relations":{"blocks":[],"blockedBy":[],"relatedTo":[],"duplicateOf":null}}
